name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: What makes EMILY's List women right for the job:
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c55db/24c69fd9/120b9f273/1894a0e0/1976394365/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEN15N_digital_email_20180207_BreakingTheMold_inact&source=WQEFEN15N_digital_email_20180207_BreakingTheMold_inact")
---

Peter,

The women EMILY's List endorses are not just pro-choice Democrats.

They're doctors, educators, business owners, veterans, community leaders, and more.

We endorse all _kinds_ of pro-choice Democratic women, because those unique backgrounds are what make our women so strong on the issues that matter the most to voters.

And that's why we need them in office where they can fight for us.

**[Donate $3 to help elect pro-choice Democratic women whose experiences make them strong candidates for office.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c55db/24c69fd9/120b9f273/1894a0e1/1976394365/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180208_email_BreakingTheMold?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+elect+prochoice+Democra&utm_campaign=WQEFEN15N_digital_email_20180207_BreakingTheMold_inact&source=WQEFEN15N_digital_email_20180207_BreakingTheMold_inact")**

Too many voices are underrepresented in office, but our women are running to change that.

For example, a woman doctor has **never** served as a Democratic representative in the House. But we've already endorsed two.

Kim Schrier (WA-08) and Mai Khanh Tran (CA-39) know how essential access to care is for their patients and were motivated to run for office after relentless Republican attacks on health care.

EMILY's List women are also educators -- including Veronica Escobar (TX-16), Chris Giunchigliani (NV-GOV), Katie Porter (CA-45), and Elizabeth Warren (MA-SEN) -- running to fight for students.

They're former business owners -- like Stacey Abrams (GA-GOV), Michelle Lujan Grisham (NM-GOV), and Marie Newman (IL-03) -- who fight for working women and families because they understand their livelihoods depend on the success of those businesses.

And our women are lifelong public servants, from veterans -- including Chrissy Houlahan (PA-06), Gina Ortiz Jones (TX-23), Connie Pillich (OH-GOV), and Mikie Sherrill (NJ-11) -- to community leaders, like Cheri Bustos (IL-17) and Sol Flores (IL-04).

Their backgrounds make them strong contenders, but we've got to help them win if we want them to be strong elected officials, too.

**Donate $3 to help make these doctors, educators, and veterans our next congresswomen, senators, and governors:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Elect-Doctors-Educators-And-Veterans](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c55db/24c69fd9/120b9f273/1894a0e2/1976394365/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180208_email_BreakingTheMold?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgElectDoctorsEduc&utm_campaign=WQEFEN15N_digital_email_20180207_BreakingTheMold_inact&source=WQEFEN15N_digital_email_20180207_BreakingTheMold_inact")**

Thank you,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

**[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c55db/24c69fd9/120b9f273/1894a0e3/1976394365/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180208_email_BreakingTheMold?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFEN15N_digital_email_20180207_BreakingTheMold_inact&source=WQEFEN15N_digital_email_20180207_BreakingTheMold_inact")**

subject: Tell Congress: Block and resist the #TrumpParade
body:
Tell Congress: Block and resist the #TrumpParade (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_parade_dems)

Petition to Congress:
"Block and resist Donald Trump’s attempts to exploit the women and men of our armed forces as political props for his authoritarian regime by holding a military parade."

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_parade_dems)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_parade_dems)

Instead of honoring the troops, Donald Trump wants them to honor him – by spending tens of millions to soothe his fragile ego by forcing troops to march by saluting.

According to recent reports, the Pentagon has already begun laying down plans for a giant, expensive military parade through Washington under direct orders from the aspiring dictator in the White House.1 Trump apparently wants to use the troops as partisan, political props in a massive #TrumpParade through the streets of our capital city so he can play dictator for a day.

Congress has the oversight and funding power to put a stop to this – but Trump will quickly brand any opposition as treasonous, so we need to create a massive public backlash against using troops as props in his #TrumpParade.

Tell Congress: Block and resist the #TrumpParade. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_parade_dems)

Trump does not care about the military or veterans. His deportation task force at ICE consistently rounds up and deports veterans who have served in the military.2 He spent his campaign attacking prisoners of wars and Gold Star parents. This parade is all about an increasingly unpopular Trump exploiting troops for his own gain. Dictators around the world use parades like these to try to scare enemies and intimidate their own citizens.

Here are a few ways we could actually honor the women and men who serve our country in uniform:

Putting a stop to deportations Ending the brinkmanship with countries like North Korea and Iran that risks military conflict Not sending them into harm’s way for needless wars like in Iraq Funding strong diplomacy so the military doesn’t need to shoulder the burden of every international flash point Paying rank-and-file soldiers better so that no military family lives in poverty Refusing to tolerate bigoted, transphobic barriers to service Reining in sky-high defense spending that helps war profiteering corporations instead of enlisted troops Spending money on aid to Puerto Rico, eliminating college debt, and providing healthcare or a thousand other ways to strengthen the country troops are willing to die to protect.
A parade honoring Trump is nowhere on that list. We cannot let Trump exploit American women and men in uniform to advance his own agenda.

Tell Congress: Block and resist the #TrumpParade. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_parade_dems)

The #TrumpParade would also cost millions of dollars at a time when Trump insists the military needs more money and his lapdog Republicans in Congress claim we cannot afford health care for children. The truth is that the military has enough money already for a dozen parades – and that money would be far better spent on schools, health care, infrastructure, diplomatic efforts, aid to Puerto Rico or a dozen other public works projects that would make America stronger as well as safer.3

Trump and Trump Republicans will claim anyone who opposes this measure opposes the troops. Some Democrats have already bravely spoken out, but others are too silent. We need to make sure Democrats see the public backlash and have the courage to oppose Trump’s dictator parade and force Republicans once again to side with Americans or with Trump.

Tell Congress: Block and resist the #TrumpParade. Click below to sign the petition:

https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_parade_dems (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_parade_dems)

Thank you for speaking out.

Murshed Zaheed, Political Director
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/trump_parade_dems)

References:

Eli Watkins and Ryan Browne, “Trump tells Pentagon to plan a military parade (https://www.cnn.com/2018/02/06/politics/military-parade-trump/index.html) ,” CNN, Feb. 7, 2018. Kristine Phillips, “The story behind this powerful photo of deported military veterans saluting the U.S. flag (https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/checkpoint/wp/2017/11/16/the-story-behind-this-powerful-photo-of-deported-military-veterans-saluting-the-american-flag/) ,” The Washington Post, Nov. 16, 2017. Win Without War, “Win Without War Denounces #TrumpParade (http://winwithoutwar.org/TrumpParade/) ,” Feb. 6, 2018.

name: The White House
subject: ‘A Nation of believers’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=285b8718cf267d0a677ad579a31d8e5e49515ab22f6d109fae55de3b99ef03ce46327e46321fe2329fc8ec19410b0316b54bc74f66a2f71f

‘A Nation of believers’

Each year for more than half a century, the National Prayer Breakfast in Washington has drawn religious leaders and members from both sides of the aisle in Congress to reflect on the importance of faith in public life.

This morning, President Donald J. Trump delivered remarks to the thousands assembled for this year’s event. He also dined last night with faith leaders and a bipartisan group of senators and congressmen ahead of today’s session. The President’s guests included Reverend Franklin Graham, Pastor Paula White, and Pastor Robert Jeffress.

“America is a Nation of believers,” President Trump said this morning. “Across our land we see the splendor of God’s creation. . . . Together as Americans, we are a tireless force for justice and for peace.”

Watch here:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=285b8718cf267d0ae471ca123950032a87f82edfb9b34eb9091c1aeddcc7ee33e60d1350251d558a7ad080a1a72049945c0ee44fd0fbd7ea
President Trump’s speech at the National Prayer Breakfast

Vice President: U.S. will unveil tough new North Korea sanctions

Vice President Mike Pence completed his first day in Japan yesterday, meeting with Japanese Defense Minister Itsunori Onodera, participating in a bilateral meeting with Prime Minister Shinzo Abe, and reaffirming the United States’ unwavering commitment to the United States-Japan alliance.

Something else the Vice President did will send a powerful message to both our allies in Asia and the international community beyond: He announced that the United States will soon unveil the toughest and most aggressive round of economic sanctions on North Korea ever.

“We will continue to isolate North Korea until it abandons its nuclear and ballistic missile programs once and for all,” Vice President Pence said. “We have brought unprecedented diplomatic and economic pressure to bear on North Korea through our maximum pressure campaign.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=285b8718cf267d0af06ed3f565d5bb19c5b5e439f99d2e2b1cdd353b0ada4aa7131eed60abefa6cfed0ef855ffdbe8382a32d7e8175a8b78
Read more about the Vice President’s trip to Japan ahead of the 2018 Winter Olympics.

Senate reaches budget deal

The headlines that broke yesterday afternoon as Senate leaders reached a budget compromise signal good news for Americans:

- Senate Reaches Two-Year Budget Deal in Hopes of Averting a Shutdown (The Weekly Standard)
- Senate reaches spending deal that includes opioid funding, CHIP extension (Modern Healthcare)
- Senate reaches budget deal with huge defense boost (Defense News)

“We are pleased that Congress has been able to meet our defense spending requirement and come together on a two-year spending bill,” Press Secretary Sarah Sanders said. “This deal achieves our top priority: a much-needed increase in funding for our national defense.”

Defense Secretary James Mattis echoed her statement. “I’ve spent the last day and a half on Capitol Hill briefing the members of Congress on our 2018 National Defense Strategy,” the Secretary said. “I’m heartened that Congress recognizes the sobering effect of budgetary uncertainty on America’s military and on the men and women who provide for our nation’s defense.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=285b8718cf267d0af678069bae79d15d111f9e2adcac410fe12fec18e883ff18b288c2b421da47458e5b543795fb1ca210a675818df66ae8
Read more from yesterday’s briefing with Sarah Sanders and Secretary Mattis.

Photo of the Day

Vice President Mike Pence and Mrs. Karen Pence arrive at Yokota Air Base | February 6, 2018 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump will meet with Secretary of Defense James Mattis.

In the afternoon, the The President will have lunch with Secretary of Veterans Affairs David Shulkin and later meet with Dr. Henry Kissinger.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=285b8718cf267d0a18e05fcdacb13adb076d99e692b5781ee2d08fa051ff159daba03a8aea919d492de1c57a1de5d5c38969fb29311f1fe8

I never thought being homeless was going to stop me from being who I wanted to be. That's why, when I went off to college, I knew I wanted to help other people get their shot at the American dream -- because that dream came true for me.

I owe a large debt to my country. I got my shot, and now it's my duty to help others get their shot.

Andrew

name: mindy@dscc.org
subject: One year later, will you keep persisting?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
One year ago, Mitch McConnell tried to cut off Elizabeth Warren on the Senate floor -- and she kept persisting anyway. Show you're standing with her and all our Democratic women >>
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180208-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Team --

After being in the trenches with Elizabeth Warren as campaign manager for her 2012 Senate campaign, I know from experience that she never backs down when fighting for our values.

So I wasn't surprised when, one year ago, Mitch McConnell tried to cut her off on the Senate floor while reading Coretta Scott King's letter that Elizabeth Warren refused. And I couldn't have been more proud when he declared:

_**" She was warned. She was given an explanation. Nevertheless, she persisted."**_
When it comes to tough fights, Elizabeth has never been one to back down. And in the last year, Elizabeth, and all our Democratic Senate women, have refused to back down in the fight to defend our values and protect working families. That's why I'm proud to be working to send ALL of them back to the Senate next year, and it's why I'm asking for your help:

[**Let 's mark the one year anniversary of "Nevertheless, She Persisted" with a gift McConnell will never forget. Contribute $1 now to the Women's Senate Network to re-elect Elizabeth Warren and HALF the women in the Senate.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180208-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Our Democratic women have faced a slew of nasty attacks from Trump and the GOP this year -- but nothing has stopped them from fiercely fighting for the future of our country.

They know what it means to persist in the face of any obstacle. And now, their determination could even enable us to win back the Senate majority this year.

It won't be easy -- we have to re-elect HALF the women of the Senate and help two more women flip red states blue -- but I know that if anyone can get the job done, it's these women, joined by supporters like you.

**One year later, show you 're still persisting in all the tough fights ahead. Pitch in $1 to the Women's Senate Network now:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/keep-persisting**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180208-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Thank you,

Mindy Myers
DSCC Executive Director

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d521/2b07114c/8956f50d/731c8210/1705554604/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. name: Team DWS
subject: Ryan's latest political spin (cutting Medicaid)
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| **"...House Speaker Paul Ryan (R-OH) appears to be attempting to repackage cuts to Medicaid, food assistance, and affordable housing as 'workforce development.' "**
_\- TalkPoverty_ ; 2/1/2018 Speaker Paul Ryan is no stranger to political spin. He's a master at it. We've seen it before. We all know Spin-master Ryan uses the term "entitlement reform" to mean "cuts to Medicare and/or Social Security." Now he's trying to use the term "workforce development" to mean "cutting Medicaid, food assistance, and affordable housing." But look, I'm just going to call it what it is: **Taking advantage of our most vulnerable.** [_**Paul Ryan isn't fooling anyone. Let him know you see through his latest attempt to roll back initiatives that are literally life-savers for millions of Americans.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f80/622b7b5b/19491ab1/307bb5b7/2467363370/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180208") [_**Add your name.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f80/622b7b5b/19491ab1/307bb5b7/2467363370/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180208") Debbie will stand up to Paul Ryan. She won't support any plan that restricts access to food or health care for low-income and middle-class families. Thank you for standing with her today. Team DWS |

Peter,

This is our year.

More than 30,000 women have told EMILY's List they want to run for office. We've never seen anything like it.

I understand firsthand the courage you need to put your name on the ballot -- I've done it before. So I can tell you how important it is that we meet these 30,000 women with as much energy and grit as they've shown us.

With all of these women stepping up, we have an opportunity to change the next decade of leadership. But what we do next depends squarely on this community pitching in.

**[Donate $3 to make sure pro-choice Democratic women have the resources they need to run strong campaigns and win this year.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c55cc/24c69fd9/11f3f092f/18945b66/1707005057/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180207_email_BreakingRecords?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+make+sure+prochoice+Democrat&utm_campaign=WQEFEN11N_digital_email_20180208_RecordNumberWomen_inact&source=WQEFEN11N_digital_email_20180208_RecordNumberWomen_inact")**

Over the past year, EMILY's List has been a proud leader in the resistance -- recruiting and training thousands of first-time candidates.

This year, our focus turns to making sure these women can launch strong campaigns and have the resources they need to be successful.

Come next year, the halls of power will look a lot different.

We've got the candidates, now we just need the support.

name: Jane Sanders, The Sanders Institute
subject: Please watch my interview with Dr. Stephanie Kelton
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Dear Friend,
I recently had the opportunity to interview Dr. Stephanie Kelton, a well-respected economist and Founding Fellow of The Sanders Institute. We discussed the research report she wrote with three colleagues showing what would happen if we canceled all student loan debt. Dr. Kelton is known for looking at issues from every angle, so I was eager to learn more about her findings.
In our in-depth interview, I asked questions from many perspectives - that of a student, a parent, a grandparent, an educator and, to be honest, a skeptic. It seemed too good to be true. Would this be too costly for taxpayers? What effect would it have on the economy as a whole? I left persuaded that policymakers should consider her findings - tested and re-tested by many outside sources - very seriously.
**[WATCH THE VIDEO»](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ad07/bed43eb/4bbef59c/75fed917/1408997280/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com")**

Part of the mission of the Sanders Institute is to explore bold new solutions to the most important issues affecting our society. This is one such exploration. I hope you'll find the interview as informative as I did.
You can also [read the summary of the report](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ad07/bed43eb/4bbef59c/75fed916/1408997280/VEsE/ "http://https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/the-macroeconomic-effects-of-student-debt-cancellation") on The Sanders Institute website. If you like what you find, please share the information and links far and wide.
Thank you for staying engaged,
Dr. Jane O'Meara Sanders
Founder/Fellow, The Sanders Institute
**[DONATE»](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ad07/bed43eb/4bbef59c/75fed911/1408997280/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sandersinstitute?refcode=em180208")**
PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
131 CHURCH STREET
BURLINGTON, VT 05401

The Sanders Institute is a tax-exempt 501(c)(3) organization.
131 Church Street, Burlington, VT 05401

**[Listen -- It's crucial we have the resources to take on last-minute Republican efforts to buy this election. Contribute $5 now to support our Special Election Fund >>>](http://act.floridadems. Friend,

We're just days away from the House District 72 special election -- [**and we urgently need your help.**](http://act.floridadems. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fdp-special-election-fund-2018?amount=5&recurring=true&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-+and+we+urgently+need+your+help&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180208_1_2&source=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180208_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180208_1_2")

Our Democratic candidate, Margaret Good, is running strong. But she needs our support to fight off the GOP cash until election day on February 13.
